sections.paragraph:
const. מַרְאֵה with suff. מַרְאֵיךְ Son 2:14 מַרְאֵהוּ Lev 13:34 מַרְאֶהָ Lev 13:35 but more often with forms of the suffix, which appear to be pl., of which however the Yod is radical (see מַעֲשֶׂה , מִקְנֶה , and Gramm. § 90, 9), like מַרְאַיִךְ Son 2:14 מַרְאָיו Job 41:1 and מַרְאֵיהֶם, מַרְאֵיהֶן (which are found construed with a sing. Gen 41:21 Lev 14:37 Dan 1:15), plur. const. מַרְאֵי Ecc 11:9 כתיב (מַרְאֵה קדי) m. (from the root רָאָה).

(1) appearance, look, aspect, Gen 41:21 Son 2:14 [and often Arab. مَرْآةُ]; Lev 13:12, לְכָל־מַרְאֵה עֵינֵי הַכֹּהֵן “according to all the looking of the priest,” i.e. as to what the priest sees in him. Deu 28:34, מַרְאֵה עֵינֶרךָ “what thine eyes behold;” verse 67 Deu 28:67 Isa 11:3 Eze 23:16.

(2) vision, sight, Exo 3:3 Eze 8:4 11:24 43:3 Dan 8:16.

(3) form, appearance, Exo 24:17 Eze 1:16, 28 Eze 1:28. It is placed after in the genitive, יְפַת מַרְאֶה Gen 12:11 טֹובַת מַרְאֶה Gen 24:16 26:7 , fair of form; and with לְ prefixed, נֶחְמָד לְמַרְאֶה beautiful of form, Gen 2:9. In the prophetic style the appearance of anything, is what is like such a thing; comp. דְּמוּת No.3. Dan 10:18, וַיִּגַע־בִּי בְּמַרְאֵה אָדָם “there touched me as the appearance of a man;” Eze 8:2 also 1:26 , דְּמוּת בְּמַרְאֵה.
